content: I can't log in to the desktop client having registered with a google account, this is apparently an issue that will be fixed in the next version of the desktop client: https://github.com/zulip/zulip/issues/13613
is there some password recovery flow? since it has your email address now.
That's not the problem, I know my password. Google flags the Zulip client as an insecure browser and blocks the login
With apparently no way to override it, our google overlords insist they know better than me
That's strange. I logged in using my Google account.

This happened to me. I was able to resolve it by going through the "forgotten password" procedure on the Zulip login page, which resulted in me having a separate password exclusive to Zulip, and I could log in using that instead of Google.

(But the Mac client seems to be just a wrapper around a browser anyway - it's identical to using Chrome, with the exception that undo doesn't work in text entry boxes.)

Nathaniel Virgo [said](https://categorytheory.zulipchat.com/#narrow/stream/229122-meta/topic/google.20acct.20.2B.20client.20.3D.20.3A(/near/191633011):

(But the Mac client seems to be just a wrapper around a browser anyway - it's identical to using Chrome, with the exception that undo doesn't work in text entry boxes.)

That must be why it worked for me.
This might be old news now, but I've just discovered that the client was updated and this issue was fixed
Perhaps this thread should be renamed to "Browsers".

just a note: Zulipchat seems to work a lot better since I updated to Safari shipped with Big Sur. (Perhaps it is an accidental relation, and it was Zulip that was updated?). In any case I used to have problems reading the messages here on my ~A4 sized vertical screen as the menu bars did not disappear, making the fonts very small. It all displays very well now.
